article_title: Former Apple Engineer Launches $3,800 Heat Pump That Installs in Under an Hour, Targeting California's Electrification Gap

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: Former Apple Engineer Launches $3,800 Heat Pump That Installs in Under an Hour, Targeting California's Electrification Gap
content_preview.summary: Merino Energy debuts the Mono, a single-unit heat pump that plugs into a standard 120-volt outlet and costs roughly half the price of a conventional mini-split, as California races to install millions of heat pumps by 2030.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Merino Energy, a Bay Area startup founded by former Apple engineer Mary-Ann Rau, began taking pre-orders on April 7 for a compact heat pump that costs $3,800 installed and can be set up in under an hour. The product, called the Mono, consolidates the indoor and outdoor components of a traditional heat pump into a single wall-mounted unit roughly the size of a radiator, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/why-a-former-airpods-engineer-is-now-building-heat-pump...
